_metadata.note: Written to repository root because the .geninsights/ directory does not exist as a physical filesystem directory. Intended location is .geninsights/analysis/uml_analysis.json.

class_diagrams.name: Module & Data Model
class_diagrams.scope: Full system — module structure, data records, operation enumeration, framework dependency
class_diagrams.description: Depicts the logical structure of app.py as a single CalculatorApp module entity containing the three functional blocks as operations. CalculatorInput and CalculationResult represent the primitive data variables flowing through the script. The Operation enumeration constrains the four arithmetic choices. StreamlitRuntime represents the full set of Streamlit API calls the module depends on.

CalculatorApp.note: Logical representation of the app.py module as a whole; not a formal Python class

CalculatorInput.note: Logical data record representing the primitive variables captured from the st.form widget group

CalculationResult.note: Logical data record representing the primitive variables computed and displayed in the output block

Operation.note: Logical enumeration of the four values enforced by the st.selectbox widget (BR-003)

StreamlitRuntime.note: External framework dependency; all 13 Streamlit API calls used by app.py are represented as operations

relationships.label: uses
relationships.description: CalculatorApp delegates all UI rendering, input capture, error display, and execution control to StreamlitRuntime

relationships.label: captures via form
relationships.description: calculator_form_block() populates the CalculatorInput data record from st.form widgets

relationships.label: produces on submit
relationships.description: calculation_and_output_block() populates the CalculationResult data record when submitted=True

relationships.label: constrained by
relationships.description: The operation field of CalculatorInput is constrained to Operation enum values by BR-003 via st.selectbox

relationships.label: drives computation
relationships.description: num1, num2, and operation from CalculatorInput determine result and symbol in CalculationResult

sequence_diagrams.name: Successful Calculation Flow (Happy Path)
sequence_diagrams.workflow: WF-001 steps 1-4 then 7-8 — full happy-path calculator interaction
sequence_diagrams.description: Traces the complete execution path for a valid calculation. Illustrates Streamlit's reactive re-run model (script executes twice: once on page load with submitted=False, once on form submit with submitted=True). Shows the st.form batching behaviour (BR-009) and a sample Multiply operation (BR-006, BR-010).

phases.name: Initial Page Load
phases.description: Streamlit executes the script with submitted=False. All widgets render with defaults. No calculation block executes.

phases.name: User Input and Form Submission
phases.description: User enters operands and selects Multiply, then clicks Calculate. Streamlit re-runs the script with submitted=True.

phases.name: Operation Dispatch and Result Display
phases.description: BR-008 dispatches to the Multiply branch (BR-006). result=50.0, symbol=x. st.success() and st.expander() render the output (BR-010).

sequence_diagrams.name: Division-by-Zero Error Flow (Error Path)
sequence_diagrams.workflow: WF-001 steps 1-4 then 5-6 — BR-001 guard triggers st.error + st.stop
sequence_diagrams.description: Traces the execution path when a user selects Divide with a zero denominator. After submission the else branch handles Divide; BR-001 immediately checks num2==0 and calls st.error() then st.stop(), halting all further script execution. No result, no expander, and no st.success() is ever reached.

key_events.description: num2 == 0 evaluates to True for Divide operation
Red error banner rendered: Division by zero is not allowed.
Streamlit halts all further script execution — no result or expander is rendered

use_case_diagrams.name: System Use Cases
use_case_diagrams.description: Maps two system actors (End User and Streamlit Runtime) to all eight use cases identified from the source code. Conditional post-submission branching from WF-001 is shown via labelled edges (success path vs BR-001 triggered path).

actors.name: End User
actors.description: The person operating the calculator in a web browser

actors.name: Streamlit Runtime
actors.description: The web framework serving and executing app.py; enforces system-level widget constraints

use_cases.name: Open Calculator App
use_cases.outcome: Calculator form displayed with default values (0.0, 0.0, Add)

use_cases.name: Enter First Operand
use_cases.outcome: Numeric float captured in num1 variable

use_cases.name: Enter Second Operand
use_cases.outcome: Numeric float captured in num2 variable

use_cases.name: Select Arithmetic Operation
use_cases.outcome: One of Add/Subtract/Multiply/Divide captured in operation variable

use_cases.name: Submit Calculation
use_cases.outcome: Script re-executes with submitted=True; branches to success or error path

use_cases.name: View Calculation Result
use_cases.outcome: Green st.success banner shows formatted equation (num1 symbol num2 = result)

use_cases.name: Expand Computation Details
use_cases.outcome: Collapsible st.expander panel reveals structured dict: first_number, second_number, operation, result

use_cases.name: Receive Division-by-Zero Error
use_cases.outcome: Red st.error banner displayed; script halted via st.stop(); no result shown
